commit_scope: Move the closure-capturing-runtime-data 'wall' into the fkwu floor. The emitted fourth-arm walker carried named higher-order dispatch but not lexical closures, so factory closures (e.g. a handler bound to a learned model) only ran three-way. Per the fourth-arm-bands.txt law (a divergence is a bug to fix, never a band left unregistered), this is closed in the walker + flattener: a closure is now a partial application (fn-index, arity, captured-values), the indirect-call arm prepends the captured tail to the call args, and the flattener lifts a factory body to a top-level function whose leading params are the captured outer params. Single-level data-capturing factory closures now cross four-way; named refs are unchanged (purely additive).

roles: principle: no permanent walls, move the gap into the floor

evidence_refs: Root cause: form-flatten.fk flt-var3 lowered a name to flt-fref [fn-index, arity] with NO captured environment, and flt-form2/flt-do had no nested-defn case -- so a factory (do (defn inner ...) inner) broke the flatten (fourth=0). The three reference kernels have a native VClosure; fkwu did not.
Walker (Form spec): hati-os-kernel.fk fkm-icall now appends the ref's captured tail (tail (tail fv)) to the call args before dispatch (fkm-cap-append).

Walker (emitted C): hati-os-kernel-emit.fk tag-44 now reads caps = fk_ht[p44t], reverses and prepends them onto the call-args list (comb = captures ++ args), then packs by arity. Empty tail => comb == args, so named refs are unchanged.

Flattener: form-flatten.fk flt-factory-parse detects (do (defn inner (p..) body) inner), compiles inner with params = captured-outer-params ++ inner-params, and emits the outer body as flt-make-closure (fk-cons fn-index (fk-cons arity capture-access-chain)); flt-sig-scan already indexed the nested inner, so fns/rbodies stay aligned.

PROVEN four-way: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/kernel-http.fk form-stdlib/affine-train.fk form-stdlib/model-service.fk form-stdlib/tests/model-handler-band.fk -> 7; fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way; 1 ok, 0 divergent. The model-capturing handler closure (ms-predict-handler bound to weights) serves the prediction on the emitted walker.
Before/after: model-service-band fourth leg went 7 -> 31 (bits c3,c4 -- the handler closure -- now pass on fkwu).

NO REGRESSION: higher-order-fn-arg 1111, bootstrap-host 4095, form-pe-coff 511, affine-train 31, presence-loop 1023 -- all still four-way, 0 divergent; a plain band-level defn band crosses four-way (20).

NEXT RUNG (owned): multi-level closures (a closure that captures and CALLS another closure -- the layer-wrap/stamp/gate middleware) do not yet cross; closure-of-closure faults the emitted walker. model-service-band (255) and presence-service-band (127) stay three-way until that lands.

commands: Git Bash: export PATH="$PATH:/c/Program Files/LLVM/bin"; cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/kernel-http.fk form-stdlib/affine-train.fk form-stdlib/model-service.fk form-stdlib/tests/model-handler-band.fk
Git Bash: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/form-pe-coff.fk form-stdlib/tests/form-pe-coff-band.fk  (regression: 511)
fourth_arm_outputs: model-handler fks lane: fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way; 1 ok, 0 divergent; verdict 7 -- the factory closure capturing the learned model crosses fkwu

deploy_validation.notes: Walker/flattener change affects only the emitted fourth arm; the three reference kernels are unchanged.

phase_gate.reason: Single-level closures cross four-way and no band regressed; the multi-level closure rung (middleware) is the named next fix before the full service is four-way.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: The emitted fkwu walker now executes lexical closures that capture runtime data (single level), so a Form recipe that returns a function bound to learned weights runs identically across Go/Rust/TypeScript/fkwu. Closures of closures remain the next rung.
public_endpoints: local-only: Form stdlib walker/flattener and a four-way band; no public endpoint
test_flows: model-handler-band -> 7 four-way: ms-predict-handler(model) returns a handler closure capturing the weights; calling it serves the model's prediction on the emitted walker.
Additive proof: every prior four-way band unchanged; a 2-element function ref (no captures) prepends nothing, so named higher-order dispatch is identical.
